text: Deep learning has revolutionized the field of computer vision, natural language processing and information retrieval. While achieving many groundbreaking successes, deep learning models have come at the cost of efficiency. As accuracy has improved, model size has increased, and computation time and memory required to run models have also dramatically increased. As such, model efficiency must be given the same importance as model accuracy, and the challenges and techniques surrounding this new goal are not yet well understood. In this paper, we formally introduce the challenges surrounding model efficiency and give a survey of five areas surrounding model efficiency, from neural network design and training methods, to compilers, runtimes, and hardware acceleration. This paper reviews prior work in the field, and provides the reader with a guide and set of experiments with open source code to help the reader quickly implement a set of techniques to improve their training and inference time. We aim to be the first comprehensive survey of model efficiency from start to finish, while also serving as an impetus for advancing research in the field of high efficiency AI, while at the same time allowing researchers and practitioners to immediately gain tangible performance improvements.
explanation: The abstract reads like a polished, generic survey description with broad, non-specific claims; such phrasing is common in AI-generated text.
